Iron Man 2 (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $312,433,331Details
International Box Office $308,723,058
Worldwide Box Office $621,156,389
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $131,347,693
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $55,199,993
Total $186,547,686
Further financial details...

Synopsis

With the world now aware that he is Iron Man, billionaire inventor Tony Stark faces pressure from all sides to share his technology with the military. He is reluctant to divulge the secrets of his armored suit, fearing the information will fall into the wrong hands. With Pepper Potts and “Rhodey” Rhodes by his side, Tony must forge new alliances and confront a powerful new enemy.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$128,122,480 (41.0% of total gross)
Legs:2.44 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:50.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$170,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,380 opening theaters/4,390 max. theaters, 6.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $447,860,709

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 7th, 2010 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
May 7th, 2010 (IMAX) by Paramount Pictures
May 2nd, 2013 (Special Engagement) by Regal Entertainment
International Releases: April 29th, 2010 (Wide) (South Korea)
August 17th, 2018 (Wide), released as Iron Man 2 (re:2018) (Australia)
October 9th, 2024 (Limited) (South Korea)
Video Release: September 28th, 2010 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of intense sci-fi action and violence, and some language
(Rating bulletin 2116, 4/14/2010)
Running Time: 125 minutes
Franchise: Iron Man, Marvel Cinematic Universe
Cast, crew, or production detail: Archival Footage of Previous Installment, Gratuitous Cameos, Intertitle, Motion Capture Performance, Scene in End Credits, Sequel With Lead Character Recast, Voiceover/Narration
Character Appearance: Iron Man
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Moscow, Monaco, Washington, D.C.
Plot point: Adult Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent, Auto Racing, Falsely Accused, Inventor, Medical and Hospitals, Professional Rivalry, Revenge, Robot, Terminal Illness
Social setting: Cold War
Source material: Marvel Comics
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Super Hero
Production/Financing Companies: Paramount Pictures, Marvel Studios, Fairview Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

With the world now aware that he is Iron Man, billionaire inventor Tony Stark faces pressure from all sides to share his technology with the military. He is reluctant to divulge the secrets of his armored suit, fearing the information will fall into the wrong hands. With Pepper Potts and “Rhodey” Rhodes by his side, Tony must forge new alliances and confront a powerful new enemy.
